data_source_summary: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (est. 2018, amended 2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal

Q1.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN; Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6

Q2.v: Forest Department, Government of Chhattisgarh; implementation through Gram Panchayat and community participation channels

Q3.v: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (established April 1, 2018; updated 2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana State Forest Portal
Q3.src: programs.md — ANNOUNCEMENT, BUDGET, CAG, OUTCOME, SURVEY, PORTAL entries

Q4.r: The primary resource is the network of 210 registered nurseries (85 government + 125 private, registered under Forest Department authority) through which saplings are produced and then distributed. These nurseries are state-directed assets operating under a government notification, not formally notified for community self-governance or GP jurisdiction. They are state/public resources managed for state-directed environmental objectives (achieving green cover targets, carbon sequestration) rather than for state revenue. Classification A2 applies: state/public resource managed under state authority. The saplings once distributed are planted on private household land, school compounds, and institutional spaces — not on demarcated community commons. T1.1 = A2 qualifies only if T1.2 ≥ 1 (verified below at Q7).
Q4.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26 Demand for Grants No. 6; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25

Q5.v: Vrikshopan Yojana operates through 210 registered nurseries (85 government, 125 private) distributed across Chhattisgarh. Saplings covering 185 species (native, fruit, timber, medicinal) are produced at these nurseries and distributed free of cost to 185,000 households, schools, and institutions. Annual distribution target: 2.5 crore saplings (FY 2025-26); cumulative since 2018: 12.5 crore saplings. Average per household: 135 saplings. Planted area equivalent: approximately 62,500 hectares. No single demarcated commons boundary — saplings are planted at diverse private and public locations chosen by individual recipients. Cost per sapling: ₹12 (100% government-subsidised; zero cost to beneficiary). Survival rate: 65% at 1-year monitoring (above 60% target).
Q5.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; Outcome Budget 2025-26; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q6.r: No demarcated commons resource boundary exists for this scheme. The 210 nurseries are geographically dispersed across the state with no single GP resolution, survey number, GPS boundary, or watershed map identifying a commons resource area. Distributed saplings are planted at individually chosen private and public locations; no public document defines the commons boundary. The scheme tracks environmental outcomes (survival %, CO2 estimates) at aggregate state level, not at a demarcated commons boundary level. FLAG: T4.2 measurement risk triggered — ρ ratio / Vitality Index cannot be computed against a measured boundary.
Q6.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Vrikshopan Yojana Portal

Q7.r: The scheme is established by an official government notification (No. 6-VY/2018/CGN) with defined beneficiary categories (households, schools, institutions) and GP-partnered implementation. Access to free saplings is a scheme-defined entitlement available to all eligible citizens — it is not purely executive discretion (access is defined by notification and cannot be arbitrarily withheld outside the scheme framework). However, access does not rise to a statutory or constitutional right in the sense of FRA community forest rights, PESA Gram Sabha mandate, or BMC biodiversity rights. It is an administratively recognised scheme entitlement with a GP implementation role. Score = 1 (customary/administratively recognised right, not purely discretionary).
Q7.src: Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25

Q8.r: No active diversion order, mining lease, change-of-land-use notification, SEZ notification, compulsory acquisition proceeding, or equivalent encumbrance identified on the scheme's operational resources (government nurseries, distribution infrastructure) in source documents reviewed. CAG audit confirms scheme operations unimpeded. PASS.
Q8.src: CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25; Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024)

Q9.breakdown: Sapling nursery establishment: ₹20 cr; Free sapling distribution: ₹22 cr; Plantation maintenance support: ₹10 cr; Monitoring: ₹3 cr
Q9.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department)

Q10.r: Commons/public-qualifying spend: (a) Sapling nursery establishment ₹20 cr — funds creation and operation of 85 government nurseries, which are public assets. Output (saplings) is produced under public mandate and serves a state-wide distribution function; the nursery infrastructure itself is a public resource asset. (b) Monitoring ₹3 cr — tracks public environmental outcomes (sapling survival, green cover, CO2) at aggregate level; outputs are public information with no private exclusivity. Total commons spend: ₹20 cr + ₹3 cr = ₹23 cr.
Q10.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Q11.r: Private-benefit spend: (a) Free sapling distribution ₹22 cr — logistics and distribution cost whose direct output flows to individual registered households for planting on private land; the direct beneficiary of the sapling (fruit, timber, medicinal produce) is the private household on private land. (b) Plantation maintenance support ₹10 cr — provides maintenance advice and follow-up support for trees planted on individual private household land; outputs flow exclusively to private assets. Total private spend: ₹22 cr + ₹10 cr = ₹32 cr. Verification: Q10 + Q11 = ₹23 cr + ₹32 cr = ₹55 cr = Q9. Consistent.
Q11.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

Q12.interp: FAIL — θ₁ = 0.418 < 0.50 threshold. Majority of scheme expenditure (₹32 cr out of ₹55 cr, 58.2%) funds direct delivery of saplings to private household plots and private maintenance support. Commons-qualifying spend (government nursery infrastructure + monitoring) = ₹23 cr (41.8%). The scheme's fiscal architecture is primarily a private-good subsidy — providing free saplings for private land planting — rather than an investment in a shared commons resource.
Q12.src: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6

docs: Chhattisgarh State Budget 2025-26, Demand for Grants No. 6 (Forest Department) — primary budget document with component breakdown
CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 — confirms ₹55 cr allocated, ₹52 cr spent (95%); verifies distribution records
Outcome Budget 2025-26 — confirms ₹55 cr budget disbursed and output metrics

Q14.r: Benefits are partially excludable. The scheme operates a beneficiary registration system: 185,000 households are registered in the scheme's enrollment database (confirmed by CAG audit). Non-registered individuals cannot access free saplings through the scheme's distribution calendar. The direct benefits (fruit, timber, medicinal produce from trees planted on private land) accrue exclusively to the registered planting household. While planted trees produce environmental externalities (CO2 sequestration, local biodiversity, microclimate) that are non-excludable, these are secondary outcomes not the primary scheme deliverable. Score = 1: partially excludable — defined registered group with practical exclusion of non-registrants. Note: exclusion is registration-based, not fee-based; no equity cross-flag on T2.2 required.
Q14.src: Chhattisgarh Economic Survey 2024-25; CAG State Accounts Report 2024-25 (enrollment database confirmed); Official Notification No. 6-VY/2018/CGN (2024); Vrikshopan Yojana Portal (participant registry — 185,000 households)
